reviews.review:
[h1]TL:DR[/h1]
[i]Be an engineer and build bridges to make people feel sketchy about where they live.[/i]

[h1]The Good Stuff[/h1]
+A fun physics based building game focused around getting people from one side to the other
+Build bridges of all types, including ones for cars, trucks, tankers and even trains
+Good set of achievements for all those hunters out there, this will give you a good time getting them all
+Get a better score by using less materials and heavier vehicles. Make everything truck proof! 

[h1]The Ok Stuff[/h1]
=Repetitive, but that's pretty clear
=The difficulty goes up as you progress providing more intuition and general common sense to complete levels (I lack the latter part there). However there does seem to be a sudden spike in difficulty after the first island, as I passed the first island without having to retry any levels due to failure
=Four types of materials to use for building, but it's random which ones you use in a level versus unlocking them for permanent use
=Not really overpriced, but since there four games in this series, try to get them on sale if you truly enjoy them

[h1]The Bad Stuff[/h1]
-The physics are iffy in this game, sometimes being entirely random where a bridge will just catapult into the air or snaps five seconds after the vehicles are completely off of it. Sometimes bridges will break when I first test them before a car touches them, and sometimes they stay just fine when the cars go on them. Like I said, entirely random at times

[h1]The Truthful Hard Facts[/h1]
[table]
[tr][td]Acquirement Method[/td][th]Bridge Constructor Bundle @ Bundle Stars[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]Price Paid[/td][th]$1.99[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]Rig Used[/td][th][url=http://steamcommunity.com/groups/thisgrouplinkwasavailable/discussions/0/1473095331487872526/]Main Computer[/url][/th][/tr]
[/table]

[h1]Check out my Steam group for more Reviews![/h1]
[url=http://steamcommunity.com/groups/thisgrouplinkwasavailable]STEAM REVIEWS GROUP![/url]

[h1]My YouTube channel![/h1]
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC4BQSDGExl23rHcd9ML2mRw?
Controls are kind of hard to use, wouldn't say I like this game. Bridge Constructor Portal and Poly Bridge are probably better options. Also they're both full versions that don't need add-ons.

that was fun for a few hours, crashes about every 45 minutes, deletes the bridge you are working on when it crashes even if youve already completed that level you need to start from scratch. I'd have a lot of fun with this if it was stable but i may never install it again.

Not a bad game to be fair but not as fun as Polybridge. Still recommend for a casual game experience.
fun

This game was okay, there are many bridge constructor games now.. I can't say I really enjoyed it after so long it just became horribly tedious.. it was okay for the first few levels though.. Don't get this one, find one of the others that adds more flavor to the game.
dead

Well thought-out, professionally designed bridge: shatters into tiny pieces if you so much as breathe on it

Crazy-ass abomination of civil engineering: can support a tank truck without breaking

10/10 realism

I have been engaged to my fiance for over a year and things were going great. We cooked together, went shopping together, loved bath and body works and made love constantly. Then he got me this for christmass, at first it was fun but then he decided that he wouldn't go inside anyone with an iq below 120. I had to complete bridge after bridge before I even got a little taste! He told me I we could do it after I built this one bridge he had trouble on but then he changed his mind  cause it was too easy! Ok, so being smarter than him isn't good enough. Btw when did figuring out bridge constructor translate into iq points?  It did feel great when I managed to managed to build a structure for trucks in bridge 6 in Tamasee and I got to carry him to bed but I used to be able to do that every night!

I was relieved when I finally finished this game and thought things would get back to normal but then he bought me the mideival version! Do you know how many bridge building physics games there are on steam?!?!
What I learned from Bridge Constructor.  Don't quit my day job or people will fall to their deaths from my shoddy engineering.

Many of the levels feel redundant. The default controls do not work ideally on a laptop. The complexity and difficulty do not scale up well as the game progresses. It can feel like building the same brige over and over again. The buget limits on most levels are so generous they remove most of the challenge. Applying personal restuctions like only using wood when steel is avaliable, or taking minimizing the buget to extreme is the only real challenge. The game would be better if the levels were more diverse, and had more meaningful restrictions. Having more complete information provided like how strong the diffrent materials are, how close members are to failing would be nice, or how heavy a load the cars are. Note: the train DLC is not included in the base game, but the slopes DLC is which is nice.

The controls in this are unacceptable. Fortunately, they're improved in the Portal game for this franchise but I wonder why on earth they think it was a good idea to give away this one as a bonus, as it's an unplayable mess in comparison and makes me want to not check out any other Bridge Constructor game.
Physics don't always make sense, somewhat buggy, bad controls

I got this game for free for buying BC Portal.

Even for free, I can't recommend this game. It's a mobile game with questionnable physics.
BC Portal is better.

A gmae with great potentials but poor implementation.
The physics based mechanics is not deterministic. You run the same bridge twice and pass or collapse randomly.
A few backgrounds are available so it feels like playing the same level over and over.

*Good game*. Had so much fun playing it.

The only thing I didn't like about it is that the music is not good, not as good as the game. But it's not a big deal.
I wouldn't waste money on this game very glitchy.
Epic bridges, decent challenges, fun times!

A Very Fun and challenging puzzle game. 
The Island nation was struck by an earthquake and destroyed every bridge on ever island... 
And as the name suggests it’s your job to re construct the fallen bridges… 
There are 64 levels in this game, all that get increasingly harder… 
Thankfully the game does a great job at teaching you the rules and physics by gradually introducing new challenges and materials… 
Every bridge has a budget, so you have to choose your materials carefully, and in some cases you only get to use a handful of these materials increasing the challenge… 
And as you construct you can test the stability of your bridge at any moment by watching a car or truck drive across it..

Game is a blast to both play and watch. You may see a slight variation when you run the test a second or third or fourth time but if you watch closely you'll see that the bridge settles and sometimes bounces just slightly so this variation happens when you hit play right away. You can actually make that part of the strategy. Subtle details like this make this very simple game, very cool!
BRIDGES
A great game for the money.
Ok game to pass the time
Very good game, lots of challenging bridges with wonderful crashes.
Not a bad game, but is only about as entertaining as a free mobile game.

11/10

would bridge again

A basic physics simulator, involving bridges, weight, stability, and cars.

There are only so many times one can construct a bridge before it becomes repetitive, and the task loses its meaning.

Not the most enthralling game I've ever played.

- If you enjoy puzzles this could be considered as fun, it's just that I don't particularly enjoy puzzles, and am questioning why I purchased it.

- There are no outstanding visual feats here, so it's best not to expect that.

(I like to imagine little people screaming inside their cars as they hurtle into the air when I build an inappropriate bridge and it collapses.

Or how many cups of coffee and sandwiches I have ruined for unsuspecting lorry drivers.)

...Perhaps it's just me who enjoys imagining that.

- Maybe if you have a crappy PC at work you could play this (if flirtatiously winking at the cute girl/guy across the corridor from you isn't working out.)

Personally, I'd stick to the excessive eye spasms route.
I have had a lot of fun with this game so far. Great strategys to the bridge building and really enjoyable overall. I would recomend this game to anyone with a few dollars to spare.
Fun physics simulator! Felt quite done with it after about 6 hours though.

A fun game.

So just casually sit back, build something (that slightly resembles a bridge), watch it go down and laugh at all the work deaths you just caused...

CLICK A THING 
CLICK A THING
CLICK A THING 
VOILAAA !!!!!!

BOB BUILDER COME TO TOWN
i make-ada great bridge for da peeps to cross, but da great bridge break-o

Very fun game. My child loves coming home everyday from school just to play this game. Its a little unfortunate that its tearing away me and his relationship because its the last pint of family I have after my parents were gunned down in a shopping mall and my wife was stolen and put into sex trafficing. At the very least its allowing him to cope with the loss of both his arms and legs in a bombing that happened at out local JC Penney. Anyway enough about me and more about this game. Its very fun and I finally got a chance to play it after smothering my son to death. It really gets your noggin joggin when you're trying to figure out how to make a bridge. One of my favorite features are the sound and music which were promptly both muted by me as soon as I started the game. But this game has a deep meaning that relates to the deep meaning of life. It's very depressing to watch the lifeless vehicles drive into a valley to their doom and as a side note this really makes me sad. The drivers of these vehicles could have families that they will never see again. I also love the variety of materials I get for building bridges like wood, steel, cable, and one other that I can't think of off the top of my head because there were just too many to even count. But the most important feature has got to be truck proofing your bridges because nothing else has ever made me wish I were dead more than watching cars easily cross a bridge just for some trucks to shatter it to pieces. In conclusion I have to say Poly Bridge is the superior game although I have to wonder what kind of person would buy a bridge building game in the first place?

I am the Michael Bay of civil engineering, my bridges most of the time collapse in a spectacular fashion sending cars and trucks to their doom in the precipice below. Cables snap, supports break and tank trucks explode mid air. My creations are a sight to behold and will probably give any real civil engineer recurring nightmares but I do love them like a proud father holding up a deformed baby, "he is a special one" I say as the midwife gives me a mournful look. My favorite bridge was actually a success, at least according to the game, as I sent trucks down that bridge, the first support glowed an ominous red, when they cleared the midpoint of the bridge that support collapsed starting a slow downward movement of the left side of the bridge, that was okay though because just as the whole thing collapsed by first truck cleared the bridge and the second one was tumbling onto the road on the right side of the bridge. A spectacular success!!!

On a serious note, it's not a bad game but the physics don't feel right and the resulting bridges that succeed according to the game look like abominations you will never see in real life more often than not. Get it on a sale but don't expect realistic physics.

Fun game to kill time and listen to music.

Great time-waster.
Its great for when you're in the mood for something simple and relaxing.

Roads only slope up to 1.5 grid squares before becoming supports, and you cannot override this. Sometimes you can't even slope past 0.5

You cannot use the Tanker/3rd-tier vehicle until you beat all levels in the group.

I completed 3 & 1/2 islands from the first group using the trucks, tried the trains map and now I am bored of it.
idk
Oh man this is just bad , ABANDON ALL LAW OF PHYSICS YE WHO ENTER HERE .

It's an ok game for what it is. All the achievements work (thus all the bridges can be completed).

I recommend you wait until the game goes on sale to get it if you're interested, as at the actual price (8.99€) the game isn't really worth it. 

Somebody will probably expect this review to have more information concerning the game, but there isn't to say. You build bridges for cars, trucks and tankers. You get a budget, and depending on how well you do, you get a higher or lower score. 

The devs did release quite a lot of free updates (Easter, Christmas, Halloween).

EDIT: A problem that came up a few times is a pink-screen. Close the game, and choose a different graphical setting (instead of fantastic, switch to good, or whatever). If this doesn't fix the issue, streaming Steam videos at the same time as launching this game will also give you a pink screen. Close the video, launch the game, and then launch the video. This fixed my issue.

11/10 would become bridge architect

Purchased this game on offer for just under £2 (Sterling) and what a bargain it is at that price.
The three things I enjoy most about this puzzle game are:
1. You need at least half a brain (as opposed to the blind luck required for a certain saga about crushing candies).
2. There is more that one solution to each puzzle.
3. It is a great example of a simple premise executed very well.

This review was written at the time of having played for around 4 hours. In this time I have completed the main islands, but not the extra islands. Therefore, I would recommend waiting for the game to be on offer for less than £5.
I like bridges.

[h1][b]★★☆☆☆[/b][/h1]
My enjoyment of the game was always covered in the shadows of the rubble that was left by my [b]im[/b]ploded bridges.

That's actually not solely a joke. Bridges and vehicles often times fly straight up; wood that should break, just fly of in a unrealistic direction. These physics is where the problem of this game lies. Since the game is all about physics, I can't recommend the game to anyone when that crucial part doesn't work.

Multiple reviewers on Steam listed out a great array of examples of how inconsistant and unrealistic the mechanics are. You might be able to ignore this, as I tried to, but getting stuck on a map only to discover in guide that the reason you were failing was that you were using TOO MUCH wood, is just aggrevating. I discovered (for myself) a consistant, succesful type of bridge that would work most of the time, as long as the cables were available. Later on, I noticed that guides were using the same type.

The type: a wooden bridge with just one or two lines of cables paralel going under it, saving an amazing amount of money everytime. It's also completely unrealistic, considering I've never seen a bridge like this in real life. When you do try and make a realistic looking bridge, which every player undoubtedly has tried, it would just break down and insulting you by doing so. For reference, look at the screenshots above. These won'l work on normal or hard difficulty (this is easy, as you can see cars, not trucks).

Despite the great amount of levels you get for the price ( I paid 5 bucks including all DLC), I just couldn't bare more of the game after completing the main and first islands. My suggestion is check out Poly Bridge.

This game is wonderful. It's casual and relaxing, yet quite challenging.

The gameplay is simple and easy to learn, but the physics can be kind of tricky. I am most of the way through the first set of islands (or whatever they're called) and I'm still discovering new ways to assemble my bridges.

My only complaint is that the materials you can use are changed up with every puzzle. In other words, you don't "earn" the advanced materials and keep them as you continue play. The game just artificially constrains what you can use, so that's kind of annoying.

Simple, fun and very addictive! Great time killer while listening to a podcast.

Would recommend if on sale!

How do I Best Describe the Game.
Imagine you’re an architect and you have been hired by a strange country that has no bridges to design and build all the bridges for them with a set budget for each "level" and an endless supply for test vehicles.
the game is just a click and drag, select the pieces for the bridge and click the area to start building then drag them out to when you need them... repeat till you have a bridge built then go see if it can get cars/trucks/trains/Santa to get over it, or more fun to see it fail.

Things to Know.
Don’t worry you can edit the bridge even if your bridge breaks down completely.
The suspension cable can solve almost any level (if you’re allowed to use them).
You can edit position and length of any parts by holding down on a link and moving it to your new place (it only stays in the new place if it’s a valid position).

Last Point.
Give each level a good go before you look up a guide, you might find you can get that train over that 700m gap with nothing but wood planks.

29 Hours played; had a good time on it and even surprised myself when some of my ideas worked first time.
Better than Call Of Duty: Infinite Warfare - [b]5 / 7[/b]

I found this game sitting in my Humble Bundle library and I have no idea when I got it. It might have been a gift. I decided to give it a try and it is ... okay. I guess. There is two serious problems, however. 

Firstly, the physics is wonky. I created a bridge which held up for the most part, but failed close to the end. I went to reinforce the part that failed, but I needed to know exactly what part failed first. When I reran the trucks across the bridge a second time, it worked perfectly. I then noticed this happening a lot. You could rerun a bridge if it barely failed over and over again and sometimes you would get lucky. 

Secondly, there are better bridge builder games out there. Poly Bridge, for example. That game has a better physics and more variation in the puzzles. 

I would only pick it up if you can get it on sale. Even then, I would recommend looking at the competition first.

Very nice and relaxing game. I like to play this game when I want to get relaxed and when i'm tipsy.  Apart from that, the physics are really well implanted. Overall a very good game for people who want to build a bit and relax.

Simple little game you can enjoy on a rainy day when nothing better to do.

Gameplay is simple to learn, and the graphics are nothing to write home to mom about.

There are times where you will create a functional bridge, and the next day you cannot recreate it.

Considering there are many similar games, on pc and phones, I find it hard to justify the price.
However, if you have young ones, this may be something they will enjoy.

this game was fun for the first little bit but after a while it got very old and repettitive like when i was playing and the bridge broke or worked i didnt care and i usually like games when i build things but this one didnt make me exited.
While it's not exactly gripping, this game is a bit of fun to pass the time with. I liked the budget limit and the building controls, although at times it can be quite tricky.

so there are a few annoyances with this game that prompted me to give this game a thumbs down

- the bridge building physics are bad (you basically cant reinforce any joints with more braceing because more often then not trying to brace a joint will actually make it weaker because instead of diverting the strain elsewhere like it would do in real life the game reads it as there is now more weight on that joint and therefore the piece that was already strained is more likely to break)

-when one piece breaks it doesn't matter if any other part of the bridge could or would be able to take the load, they all are suddenly unstable and brittle and WILL break

- to compound on the inability to brace joints they also have very few "braced points"(and or starting placement points" in this game) which with only having 1 point on many levels on each side of the gap makes it increasingly difficult to make any type of stable bridge 

-the wood and metal beams must be made of a heavy paper because they can hardly hold up their own weight (bridges HAVE to be minimalist in function because of the weight of the materials is too heavy for their strength basically trying to have a huge bridge is impossible)

- building the bridges has a few glitches, quite often like every 20th click when placing a bridge piece (not deleting it) it will delete any other pieces located in that area. about every 30th click your delete button will not regester no matter now many times you click with it until you then click on a available building material

so considering what i've complained about is the MAIN MAJORITY of the entire gameplay I would say even buying this for 3$ I am not satisfied and that I don't recommend it just wasn't worth the time
Not good :(
love this game just to spend some fun time!

The objective of this game is that, with as little budget as possible, car/truck/tanker can successfully cross the (incomplete) bridge you build. Yeah, you can build an ordinary triangle-shaped bridge and move onto the next stage, but the real fun is that you build an unconventional, yet cheaper bridge; you can make cars fly, make only one of two cars reach the end, etc. It's really surprising for palyers to do numerous things with just a simple game aspects, but with a great idea.

[h1]The good[/h1]
+ Fun basic gameplay (the tension! the explosions!)
+ Decent production quality
+ LOTS of maps

[h1]The bad[/h1]
- Difficult to complete without guides (even with guides there seem to be one level that just doesn't work for me)
- Occasional weird physics behaviour - trucks falling through bridge elements and staying suspended by their wheels and such

[h1]Verdict[/h1]
If the basic theme sounds fun to you, give it a try :) There's no better feeling than after barely getting through, the game giving you a golden badge while your grand creation completely collapses in the background :3

It's OK... Music sounds kind of sad, and the sounds are a little bit annoying. Gets a bit repetitive, but feels fun overall. Achievements and unrealistic physics can be entertaining. Wait for a sale. Worth it for $5 or so.
Hours spent on fun

For people who love simulation & puzzle fans its good. You really need to find the mistakes and need to try many different solutions of how you build and with what. 54 Achievements are good, but its a hard fight to get them all, the game could have more achievements (each level would be amazing), but its fine. 5 Trading Cards missing. For hardcore simulation/ puzzle fans i give 75 % because its really tough to find solutions without guides/ help but most time you need 2-3 ways of how to build and only change them a bit to the level. For achievement hunters i give 80 % for 54 in 15 hours, 50 % for normal gamers, you like it or not. Last thing buy it with a discount 10 euros for 15 hours maybe looks okay, but maybe you stop gaming after 3-5 hours because you dislike it, so get it for 66-75 % discount. Thx 4 reading.

Well, I remember back in the day I played a similar game called Pontifex. I remember enjoying that game but I can't say the same for this one. Much of the challenge comes from finding the right spot for triangle supports (typically around the whole bridge so that lone triangle supports don't break it) and levels seem very similar to each other. It gets boring after some time.

Fun wears of really quickly. Instead of challenging the game keeps repeating itself and becomes quite boring. Completing the puzzles doesn't feel rewarding at all but more like a chore. I played because I payed. Not recommended unless it's 99 cents or less.

Simple bridge constructing game with mobile game UI and bad physics. Offers entertainment for approx. two hours, and with its price at (9€) I can't really recommend it. If you insist on buying a bridge construction game, I would recommend buying Poly Bridge instead. (12€ atm)
Its a good game to play when ur bored. 10/10

I wanted to like this one, as it seemed like a fun little game to kill the time. But:

- Physics are awful, and inconsistent.
- There's a complete lack of tips, explanations or how to; you're left trying things again and again and again for no apparent reason. Which goes for everything basically, because...
- There's a fair bit of repetition, and unwanted repetition at that: did you finally understand how the game wants you to use wooden planks when you've got noplace to lean them on? Great, now do it again a couple of times, with minor changes that you have to figure out yourself by failing again and again and again. And that's my biggest complaint:
- It's limited. What you can construct is limited. What you can imagine is limited, down to minor details, and these details are so hardcoded into each level that they take away all the fun.

Nope. Thanks but no thanks.

There is no strategy at all to this game you just have to spend hours on the harder levels esting random things until you break the game and get it to work. if your looking for a game similar to this but much better and more fun just play poly bridge.

A really fun game, it have a lot of creativity on the levels, and a lot of challenging too, also pretty funny in some occasions, but dont expect realistic physics.

[h1]Pros[/h1]
[olist]

[*]Challenging levels.

[*]Being limited by money and materials make the game more entertaining and challenging.
[*]Foments creativity.
[/olist]
[h1]Cons[/h1]
[olist]
[*]Lack of options make it seems like a flash game.
[*]Unrealistic physics system.
[*]The difficulty between levels is not consistent.
[/olist]

Never trust a bridge you find in Camatuga.  Go well out of your way to find a ferry, or go all the way around the river to get to the other side, but don't go across a bridge!

Bridge Constructor is a game where you build bridges in the fictional lands of Camatuga.  Your budget, and sometimes your available supplies (wood, steel, concrete, and rope) are limited, which means that sometimes you have to get creative.  But beyond that, it's all about figuring out how you can build a bridge, on budget, that will carry across cars, then medium trucks, then tanker-trailers.

While it may sound like a fairly straightforward physics building game, the wacky bridges you'll often wind up with and the explosions of wood and steel when things don't quite work out, make it hilarious.  The game is both a lot of fun by yourself, but also a lot of fun with friends, especially towards the more difficult later levels, where you can pass around the mouse and try your own approaches to building spans on low budgets.  Inevitably it will take a lot of tries to get it right, and laughter will ensue as unlikely bridges collapse - or occasionally prove just good enough to support the load, despite their "I'd never drive across that in real life" appearance.

Among the Bridge Constructor series, I'd put this one and Medieval at pretty much equal for gameplay, as Medieval introduces some interesting new elements, but the three vehicles types per bridge extends the challenge, in a good way, of each bridge here (while not preventing you from moving to the next one as long as you have cars getting across).  However, this iteration gave me much more playtime before I'd completed most of the game, and they're the same price, so my recommendation would be to get Bridge Constructor, have a blast, and if you're still wanting more, picking up Medieval after that.
This bridge game is fun and has lots of levels (free christmas and easter ones included). As i bought the bundle in discount, it's really worth the price !
10/10 Would bridge again!
